perturbation.original_text: The Reseller shall use all commercially reasonable efforts to market, promote, distribute, and sell the Products to Customers in the Territory, and shall, on its own account, provide a trained and competent sales and marketing team for the efficient promotion and sale of the Products.
perturbation.changed_text: The Reseller shall use all commercially possible efforts to market, promote, distribute, and sell the Products to Customers in the Territory, and may, on its own account, provide a sales and marketing team for the promotion and sale of the Products.
perturbation.explanation: The change of 'commercially reasonable efforts' to 'commercially possible efforts' elevates the required level of effort, creating an in-text conflict. Furthermore, the wording was changed from 'shall, on its own account, provide a trained and competent sales and marketing team for the efficient promotion and sale of the Products' to 'may, on its own account, provide a sales and marketing team for the promotion and sale of the Products.' which now makes the the reseller to only potentially provide sales and marketing team. The original text imposes a duty to provide a trained and competent team, whereas the altered text makes providing any team discretionary. This leads to in-text contradiction regarding Reseller's commitment of marketing, and the level of commitment the reseller needs to put in to sale the product.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Reseller shall use all commercially possible efforts to market, promote, distribute, and sell the Products to Customers in the Territory, and may, on its own account, provide a sales and marketing team for the promotion and sale of the Products. The Reseller shall achieve the commercialization milestones by the dates set forth in the Commercialization Timetable attached hereto as Exhibit C.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates 'commercially reasonable efforts' and the provision of a 'trained and competent sales and marketing team.' The changed text weakens the effort standard to 'commercially possible efforts' and makes the provision of a sales and marketing team discretionary ('may'). This creates a direct in-text contradiction, as the Reseller's commitment is no longer as strong or obligatory, contradicting the stated intention of achieving commercialization milestones as given in 'Contradicted Text.'
